input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install latest .NET SDK via winget
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8

# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# Discover available workloads dynamically
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows targets)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target requires WinUI/WinAppSDK)

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or for build tools only:
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add required components:

```powershell
# Modify VS installation with required workloads
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --passive --norestart
```

## 4. Android SDK Setup

```powershell
# MAUI installs Android SDK automatically, but to manage manually:

# Find where MAUI put the Android SDK
dotnet build -t:ResolveAndroidTooling -v:n 2>&1 | Select-String "AndroidSdkPath"

# Default location:
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"

# List installed packages
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Discover latest platform/build-tools dynamically
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>&1 |
  Select-String "platforms;android-" | Sort-Object | Select-Object -Last 5

& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>&1 |
  Select-String "build-tools;" | Sort-Object | Select-Object -Last 5

# Install specific versions (example)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0" "platform-tools"
```

## 5. Accept Android Licenses

```powershell
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
# Answer 'y' to all
```

## 6. Set Environment Variables

```powershell
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk", "User")
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  (Get-ChildItem "$env:ProgramFiles\Microsoft\jdk-*" | Sort-Object Name | Select-Object -Last 1).FullName,
  "User")
```

## 7. Enable Developer Mode (required for Windows target)

```powershell
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## 8. Create and Verify a Test Project

```powershell
dotnet new maui -n TestApp
cd TestApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# List all available target frameworks in the project
Select-String "TargetFrameworks" *.csproj
```

## 9. Run on Android Emulator

```powershell
# Create an AVD dynamically using latest system image
$img = & "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>&1 |
  Select-String "system-images;android-\d+;google_apis;x86_64" |
  Select-Object -Last 1
$imgName = ($img -split '\s+')[0].Trim()

& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "$imgName"

echo "no" | & "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "MauiTest" -k "$imgName" --device "pixel_5"

# Launch emulator
Start-Process "$androidSdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -ArgumentList "-avd MauiTest"

# Deploy
dotnet run -f net8.0-android
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net8.0` with `net9.0` if using .NET 9 (`dotnet --list-sdks` to check).
- The MAUI workload bundles OpenJDK and Android SDK — manual install is only needed for customization.
- Windows target requires WinAppSDK which ships with the MAUI workload + VS components.
